title: Incidence of HIV, ages 15-49 (per 1,000 uninfected population ages 15-49)
definition: Number of new HIV infections among uninfected populations ages 15-49 expressed per 1,000 uninfected population in the year before the period.
unit: new infections per 1,000 uninfected population ages 15-49

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: UNAIDS estimates, Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS), uri: https://aidsinfo.unaids.org/, publisher: UNAIDS, date accessed: 2025-08-27, date published: 2025-07

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Eswatini, South Africa, Mozambique, while the lightest include Jordan, Syrian Arab Republic, Afghanistan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the hiv incidence pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: HIV incidence is a distinct and geographically meaningful expert health map with clear regional signals.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Male adult mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Female adult mortality lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.
Communicable disease deaths lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: HIV indicators should be framed carefully and kept out of casual easy tiers.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 QA: distinct regional health pattern with adequate coverage and clear rate unit.
Expert-only because the topic is sensitive and should not appear in easy casual rounds.
